Variables.Name: LightingGuid
Variables.Description: Unique ID for this material, used for caching during distributed lighting

Variables.Name: NumCinematicMipLevels
Variables.Description: Number of mip-levels to use for cinematic quality.

Variables.Name: SRGB
Variables.Description: This should be unchecked if using alpha channels individually as masks.

Variables.Name: bNoTiling
Variables.Description: If true, the RHI texture will be created using TexCreate_NoTiling

Variables.Name: bUseCinematicMipLevels
Variables.Description: Whether to use the extra cinematic quality mip-levels, when we're forcing mip-levels to be resident.

Variables.Name: AssetUserData
Variables.Description: Array of user data stored with the asset

Variables.Name: CachedCombinedLODBias
Variables.Description: Cached combined group and texture LOD bias to use.

Variables.Name: CompressionSettings
Variables.Description: Compression settings to use when building the texture.

Variables.Name: bAsyncResourceReleaseHasBeenStarted
Variables.Description: Whether the async resource release process has already been kicked off or not

Variables.Name: Filter
Variables.Description: The texture filtering mode to use when sampling this texture.

Variables.Name: LODGroup
Variables.Description: Texture group this texture belongs to

Variables.Name: AdjustBrightness
Variables.Description: Static texture brightness adjustment (scales HSV value.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustBrightnessCurve
Variables.Description: Static texture curve adjustment (raises HSV value to the specified power.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustVibrance
Variables.Description: Static texture "vibrance" adjustment (0 - 1) (HSV saturation algorithm adjustment.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustSaturation
Variables.Description: Static texture saturation adjustment (scales HSV saturation.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustRGBCurve
Variables.Description: Static texture RGB curve adjustment (raises linear-space RGB color to the specified power.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustHue
Variables.Description: Static texture hue adjustment (0 - 360) (offsets HSV hue by value in degrees.)  (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustMinAlpha
Variables.Description: Remaps the alpha to the specified minmax range, defines the new value of 0 (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: AdjustMaxAlpha
Variables.Description: Remaps the alpha to the specified minmax range, defines the new value of 1 (Non-destructive; Requires texture source art to be available.)

Variables.Name: CompressionNoAlpha
Variables.Description: If enabled, the texture's alpha channel will be discarded during compression

Variables.Name: DeferCompression
Variables.Description: If enabled, defer compression of the texture until save.

Variables.Name: MaxTextureSize
Variables.Description: The maximum resolution for generated textures. A value of 0 means the maximum size for the format on each platform, except HDR longlat cubemaps, which default to a resolution of 512.

Variables.Name: CompressionQuality
Variables.Description: The compression quality for generated textures.

Variables.Name: bDitherMipMapAlpha
Variables.Description: When true, the alpha channel of mip-maps and the base image are dithered for smooth LOD transitions.

Variables.Name: AlphaCoverageThresholds
Variables.Description: Alpha values per channel to compare to when preserving alpha coverage.

Variables.Name: bPreserveBorder
Variables.Description: When true the texture's border will be preserved during mipmap generation.

Variables.Name: bFlipGreenChannel
Variables.Description: When true the texture's green channel will be inverted. This is useful for some normal maps.

Variables.Name: bForcePVRTC4
Variables.Description: For DXT1 textures, setting this will cause the texture to be twice the size, but better looking, on iPhone

Variables.Name: PowerOfTwoMode
Variables.Description: How to pad the texture to a power of 2 size (if necessary)

Variables.Name: PaddingColor
Variables.Description: The color used to pad the texture out if it is resized due to PowerOfTwoMode

Variables.Name: bChromaKeyTexture
Variables.Description: Whether to chroma key the image, replacing any pixels that match ChromaKeyColor with transparent black

Variables.Name: ChromaKeyThreshold
Variables.Description: The threshold that components have to match for the texel to be considered equal to the ChromaKeyColor when chroma keying (<=, set to 0 to require a perfect exact match)

Variables.Name: ChromaKeyColor
Variables.Description: The color that will be replaced with transparent black if chroma keying is enabled

Variables.Name: MipGenSettings
Variables.Description: Per asset specific setting to define the mip-map generation properties like sharpening and kernel size.

Variables.Name: bUseNewFilter_UE4
Variables.Description: New Tex Mip Filter  Tex MaxSize Filter from UE5

Variables.Name: CompositeTexture
Variables.Description:
Can be defined to modify the roughness based on the normal map variation (mostly from mip maps).
	  MaxAlpha comes in handy to define a base roughness if no source alpha was there.
	  Make sure the normal map has at least as many mips as this texture.

Variables.Name: CompositeTextureMode
Variables.Description: defines how the CompositeTexture is applied, e.g. CTM_RoughnessFromNormalAlpha

Variables.Name: CompositePower
Variables.Description:
default 1, high values result in a stronger effect e.g 1, 2, 4, 8
	  this is no slider because the texture update would not be fast enough

Variables.Name: bUseLegacyGamma
Variables.Description: A flag for using the simplified legacy gamma space e.g pow(color,12.2) for converting from FColor to FLinearColor, if we're doing sRGB.
